name: Örvar-Oddr
description: Örvar-Oddr, meaning "Arrow-Odd," is a legendary Norse hero whose sagas recount a life spanning over three centuries. Born to Grimr Loðinkinni and Löfthöena, his fate was intertwined with a prophecy foretelling his death at the hands of his beloved horse, Faxi. To avert this fate, his father sent Faxi away. Oddr embarked on numerous Viking expeditions and adventures, battling monstrous creatures, giants, and sorcerers. He wielded the magical arrows bestowed upon him by his foster father, Ingjald, which always found their mark. His exploits included joining the famed band of heroes led by Harald Wartooth and fighting alongside them in legendary battles like the Battle of Brávellir. He faced challenges from berserkers, dragons, and the forces of Hel herself. Despite his efforts to cheat death, Oddr's prophecy eventually came to pass when, in old age, he returned to his homeland and was accidentally bitten by the skull of Faxi, fulfilling the foretold demise. Örvar-Oddr represents the quintessential Viking hero – brave, skilled in combat, and driven by a thirst for adventure and glory. His saga embodies the Norse ideals of courage, loyalty, and the acceptance of fate, however hard one might fight against it. His story also explores themes of mortality, the power of prophecy, and the bittersweet nature of a long life filled with both triumph and loss. He is a figure of enduring appeal, embodying the spirit of the Viking age and its rich tapestry of myth and legend. His tale is told in 'Örvar-Odds saga', a late Icelandic saga.
subtitle: The Arrow-Odd, Viking Hero of Legend
achievements: Battled and defeated the giant Ögvaldr
Sailed to Bjarmaland and fought against its inhabitants
Joined Harald Wartooth's army and fought in the Battle of Brávellir

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy of Faxi
keyMyths.description: Oddr was prophesied to die from his horse, Faxi. His father sent the horse away, but the prophecy was ultimately fulfilled when Oddr was bitten by Faxi's skull.

keyMyths.title: The Magical Arrows
keyMyths.description: Oddr received magical arrows from his foster father, Ingjald. These arrows always hit their target, making Oddr an exceptionally skilled archer.

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Brávellir
keyMyths.description: Oddr fought alongside Harald Wartooth in the legendary Battle of Brávellir. This battle was a major event in Norse saga literature.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: The primary source for the legend of Örvar-Oddr.
sources.text: The primary source for the legend of Örvar-Oddr.
